accepted.reason: 2D convolution with boundary clamping is a canonical high-value optimization kernel.
Sequence symbol encode/decode and code mapping are tight loops with clear Cython speedup potential.
Supervoxel relabeling and voxel-feature extraction are loop-heavy array operations suitable for optimization.
Log-space probability arithmetic is numerically sensitive and compute-focused, making it useful training data.
Vector arithmetic/dot/normalize primitives are dense numeric operations with straightforward optimization patterns.
Grid traversal with modular indexing is a compact algorithmic loop kernel useful for optimization tasks.

skipped.reason: Heavily framework-coupled SPH class implementation depends on project internals, reducing portability as unpaired data.
Text normalization wrapper is dominated by project-specific NLP plumbing rather than standalone optimization kernels.
Contains incorrect/buggy selection-sort logic and low-quality signal for training stable optimization behavior.
C++ topology container wrapper is mostly interface/memory-management glue, not a direct compute optimization target.
